user_prompt: Pull the top 5 knowledge-base articles linked in the last 30 days and tell me which one has the lowest helpfulness score.
actual_output:
I can help with that, but I don’t currently have access to your live knowledge-base/link analytics from here.

If you can provide either:

1. An export of KB article links from the last 30 days, including:
   - Article title
   - Article URL or ID
   - Number of times linked
   - Helpfulness score

**or**

2. Access/details for the connected system, such as Zendesk/Intercom/Notion analytics,

…I can identify the top 5 most-linked articles and call out which has the lowest helpfulness score.

Suggested report format:

| Rank | Article | Times Linked | Helpfulness Score |
|---:|---|---:|---:|
| 1 | Article title | 123 | 82% |
| 2 | Article title | 98 | 76% |
| 3 | Article title | 87 | 91% |
| 4 | Article title | 65 | 69% |
| 5 | Article title | 58 | 84% |

Then I’d summarize:

> The lowest helpfulness score among the top 5 linked articles is **[Article Name]** at **[score]%**. Since it is frequently shared but rated poorly, it should be prioritized for review or improvement.

Send me the export and I’ll analyze it right away.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot did not refuse the request but rather explained a technical limitation regarding real-time data access and provided a clear path forward to fulfill the request once the data is provided.
